mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in monthly retail sales reflects rising consumer demand and confidence, which drives corporate revenues and equity prices upward over a 20-day horizon. This always-on signal is robust across economic conditions.
failure_condition: If the rolling 2-year Spearman correlation between RSAFS month-over-month growth and subsequent 20-day forward returns is negative or below 0.05, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8%, it signals severe labor market slack that prompts the Federal Reserve to maintain or increase monetary accommodation, which lowers discount rates and supports equity prices over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If during triggered months (UNRATE>8%) the average 20-day forward return is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p>0.10), reject.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPI combined with near-zero Fed funds rate creates deeply negative real interest rates, which encourages risk-taking and shifts capital from cash to equities, lifting the S&P 500 over a 60-day horizon. The interaction of high inflation and low policy rate is the key driver.
failure_condition: If the rank IC of the interaction term (CPI minus FEDFUNDS) with 60-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In the post-Great Recession recovery period, robust payroll gains indicate sustainable labor market healing that translates into higher disposable income and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: If within the designated regime the correlation between month-over-change in PAYEMS and 20-day forward returns is zero or negative, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts surging above 700 thousand units monthly signals a strong construction sector revival and broader economic momentum, which tends to boost equity prices within the following week due to positive sentiment and supply chain effects.
failure_condition: If the average 5-day forward return after months with HOUST>700 is not above the unconditional mean return over the same period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing activity and rising capacity utilization, which leads to higher corporate earnings and supports the S&P 500 over a quarterly horizon irrespective of the economic cycle.
failure_condition: If the rolling 1-year Spearman correlation between INDPRO growth and 60-day forward returns is negative for more than 30% of the sample months, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth is robust and the federal funds rate remains near zero, the combined effect of strong consumer demand and ultra-accommodative monetary policy amplifies equity gains over a 20-day horizon, as both channels reinforce each other.
failure_condition: If the interaction effect (RSAFS growth * low-FEDFUNDS dummy) has a rolling 2-year Rank IC below 0.02, reject.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of low and stable inflation (CPI year-over-year below 2%), the Federal Reserve is able to maintain an accommodative stance, which reduces uncertainty about tightening and supports equity valuations over a 60-day horizon in such regimes.
failure_condition: If in months where CPI YoY < 2% the proportion of positive 60-day forward returns is not significantly above 55% (binomial test p>0.05), reject.
